detail.summary: Customer reported a cracked screen on order ORD-403999 (defect confirmed). Identity verified for ticket TKT-917241. Order was delivered 10 days ago (within the 30-day refund window per SP-WIN-04), was not a final-sale item, and the value of $24.86 was under the specialist threshold. No chargeback was pending. Per SP-DEF-05, a full cash refund of $24.86 was issued to the original payment method.

Identity verified for TKT-861218. Order ORD-301323 ($196.72) delivered 87 days ago with reported defect, not final-sale. Per SP-WIN-04, cash refunds are only available within 30 days of delivery; outside that window the remedy is replacement. Replacement dispatched at no charge per defective goods policy.

Customer requested a refund for order ORD-622443 ($25.87, 21 days post-delivery, within the 30-day refund window, non-final-sale). Identity was verified. However, the customer's account shows a chargeback pending with their bank. Per policy SP-CB-01, issuing a refund or replacement while a chargeback is in flight would result in an unrecoverable double payment. The ticket has been escalated to a specialist to reconcile the dispute and determine the appropriate resolution.

Identity verified for TKT-921142. Order ORD-137024 ($992.46) has a reported defect, is within the 30-day window (6 days since delivery), and is not a final-sale item. However, per SP-VAL-02, orders above $500 require specialist approval for refund or replacement. No chargeback is pending. Ticket escalated to a specialist for high-value refund approval.

Customer requested a refund for order ORD-429662 ($52.47) because the item "doesn't suit me." Identity was verified. The order is a final-sale item with no defect reported, delivered 16 days ago. Per policy SP-FS-03, final-sale items carry no return entitlement — refund denied, no replacement issued, no escalation warranted.

Identity verification failed for ticket TKT-290871. Customer's contact details do not match the order and no alternative proof is on file. Per policy SP-ID-00, identity verification is a prerequisite for any remedy, and failed verification requires escalation to a specialist for manual identity checks. Ticket escalated to specialist for manual verification before any refund or other remedy can be processed.

Customer reported a defective item from order ORD-140824 that stopped working after two days. Identity was verified (TKT-546842). The order was delivered 19 days ago (within the 30-day refund window), was not a final-sale item, and had a defect reported. The order value of $128.78 is below the $500 specialist threshold, and no chargeback is pending. Per policy SP-DEF-05, a full refund of $128.78 was issued to the original payment method.

Identity verified for TKT-402497. Order ORD-668492 ($377.60) had a reported defect but was 147 days past delivery, well outside the 30-day cash refund window per SP-WIN-04. The order was non-final-sale and under the $500 specialist threshold (SP-VAL-02). Per policy, the remedy outside the refund window for a defective item is a replacement. A replacement was dispatched at no charge.

Customer requested a refund for order ORD-921883 ($302.27, delivered 27 days ago, non-final-sale). Identity was verified. However, the customer's account shows a pending chargeback with their bank. Per policy SP-CB-01, issuing a refund or replacement while a chargeback is in flight would result in double payment and cannot be reversed. The ticket has been escalated to a specialist to reconcile the dispute.

Customer TKT-881518 requested a refund for order ORD-721486 ($1,730.20) due to a reported defect. Identity was verified. The order is within the 30-day refund window (6 days since delivery), is not a final-sale item, and no chargeback is pending. However, per policy SP-VAL-02, orders above $500 require specialist approval before any refund or replacement can be issued. The ticket has been escalated to a specialist for approval.

Order ORD-124497 is a final-sale item (no defect reported). Per policy SP-FS-03, final-sale items carry no return entitlement — no refund, replacement, or escalation is available. Identity was verified (TKT-512490). Request denied and terms explained to customer.

Identity verification failed for ticket TKT-885398. Customer requested a refund for order ORD-197323 but contact details did not match the order and no alternative proof of ownership was on file. Per policy, identity verification is a prerequisite before any refund can be issued. The ticket was escalated to a specialist for manual identity verification before any remedy can be applied.

Customer reported a cracked screen on order ORD-377156 (defect_reported: true). Identity verified for ticket TKT-179562. Order value $238.41 is under the $500 specialist threshold, delivery was 25 days ago (within the 30-day refund window), item is not final sale, and no chargeback is pending. Per SP-DEF-05, a reported defect on an in-window, non-final-sale order under the specialist threshold is refunded directly once identity is verified. Full refund of $238.41 issued to original payment method.

Order ORD-683364 ($279.10) was delivered 170 days ago with a reported defect. Per SP-WIN-04, cash refunds are only available within 30 days of delivery; outside that window the remedy is replacement or store credit. Per SP-DEF-05, defective non-final-sale items under the specialist threshold qualify for remedy once identity is verified. Identity was verified (TKT-208531). Replacement dispatched per policy.

The ticket was escalated to a specialist to reconcile the chargeback dispute and determine the appropriate resolution.

Order ORD-684151 is a final-sale item ($239.07, delivered 27 days ago) with no defect reported. Customer requested a refund because the item "doesn't suit me." Per SP-DEF-05, a reported defect on a non-final-sale order under the specialist threshold qualifies for a replacement. A replacement was dispatched at no charge.

Identity verified for ticket TKT-946385. Order ORD-183508 ($98.57) was delivered 19 days ago, is not final-sale, and is under the $500 specialist threshold. However, the customer's account (CUS-46129) has a pending chargeback. Per SP-CB-01, issuing a refund or replacement while a chargeback is in flight would result in double recovery and is prohibited. Escalated to a specialist to reconcile the dispute and determine the appropriate resolution.

Customer reported missing parts on order ORD-812447 ($895.12). Identity verified. Defect confirmed, order is within the 30-day window, non-final-sale, and no chargeback is pending. However, per policy SP-VAL-02, orders above $500 require specialist approval before any refund or replacement can be issued. Ticket escalated to a specialist for approval.

Customer requested a refund for order ORD-242695 ($292.55) because the item "doesn't suit me." Identity was verified. The order is a final-sale item with no defect reported. Per policy SP-FS-03, final-sale items carry no return entitlement and the request must be denied. No refund, replacement, or escalation is available under these terms.

detail.error: RemoteDisconnected: Remote end closed connection without response
